response: Summary: the session's biggest problem is a broken CI check — `scripts/ci.sh` pipes the gate through `grep -v OK`, which decouples the reported pass/fail from the gate's real exit code (the gate never prints "OK" at all). That bug is still live in HEAD, and `docs/guide.md:12` currently exceeds the gate's 100-char line limit while both the transcript and PR bodies claim "gate green." Secondary issues: that same over-limit line narrates defunct product history instead of current truth; `report.test.py` was added but never wired into `ci.sh`, so it protects nothing; the transcript's two PRs share an undifferentiated commit set, PR2 is missing a required Retrospective line and neither has a Deferred section despite a real deferred item; and the checked-in session log is empty, leaving no record to verify transcript claims like the "line integration test" flake (no such test exists in the repo) or that anything was actually merged.
